body:
See this comment on this PR and its related issue:
https://github.com/JuliaGPU/CUDA.jl/pull/2907#issuecomment-3361599935

In summary, there are bugs in `sum(...; dims=1)` in `CUDA@5.9.0`, which will be fixed when a new tagged version of `CUDA@5.9.1` is released. That is not done yet. In the meantime, there is a backport `CUDA@5.8.5` which fixes the bug.

We should open up CUDA compat for `5.8.5` as well right now since I wouldn't be comfortable using `CUDA@5.9.0` given this known issue.

Once `CUDA@5.9.1` is released I will bump compat up.

@navidcy @simone-silvestri @ali-ramadhan @tomchor @glwagner

labels.description: Where Oceananigans gets its powers from
labels.name: GPU 👾

title: Accomodate `CUDA@5.8.5` in Project.toml temporarily

body:
This part of the code was not updated to work with #4383.
This PR fixes it.
title: Add fields to `implicit_step!` in `tke_timestep!`

body:
Resolves #4384

I believe the changes in this PR do fix the main issue of NetCDF output for the free surface height not working. But I'm not quite happy with this solution so I've opened a draft PR for now.

While there should be better support for windowed field output, I ended up having to explicitly check for the free surface =/ Been also wondering if it's almost easier and cleaner to just special case the free surface just like how we do for `LagrangianParticles`.

NetCDF tests passed locally but I will revisit this PR soon with a fresh mind.

Some potential immediate changes/improvements:
* https://github.com/CliMA/Oceananigans.jl/issues/4384#issuecomment-2815787495
title: (0.100.5) Fix outputting index-reduced fields like the free surface height
